category1.title: Can OpenAI’s ‘Master of Disaster’ Fix AI’s Reputation Crisis?
category1.summary: Global affairs chief Chris Lehane wants to tone down the deb
category1.detail: Global affairs chief Chris Lehane wants to tone down the debate over AI’s societal impacts—and get states to pass laws that won’t derail OpenAI’s meteoric rise.

category1.title: The Path, founded by Tony Robbins and Calm alums, hopes to offer safer AI therapy
category1.summary: The Path says its AI model has scored 95 on the mental healt
category1.detail: The Path says its AI model has scored 95 on the mental health safety AI benchmark, Vera-MH. This compares to a top score of 65 for the consumer bots.

category1.title: Hark raises $700M Series A for its secretive ‘universal’ AI interface
category1.summary: Hark expects to release its first multimodal models this sum
category1.detail: Hark expects to release its first multimodal models this summer, which it says will power a personal AI platform that works with existing products and services. The company expects to follow that with hardware devices built specifically for those systems.

category1.detail: Anthropic has told its investors that it will more than double revenue to around $10.9 billion in its second quarter.

category1.title: I Cloned Myself With Gemini’s AI Avatar Tool. The Result Was Unnervingly Me
category1.summary: I used the Gemini app to generate lifelike videos featuring
category1.detail: I used the Gemini app to generate lifelike videos featuring a digital clone of myself. Google sees this as the future of creation. I’m still creeped out.

category2.detail: The AI-powered audiobook generation won't bind authors to an exclusive contract, meaning they are free to publish their generated audiobooks anywhere.

category2.detail: One of the most promising introductions at Google’s I/O developer conference on Tuesday was a new way for consumers to use the web: AI agents. Unfortunately, it was also the most confusing.

category3.detail: AMD has announced that its 6th Gen EPYC processor, codenamed Venice, has entered production ramp on TSMC's N2 process in Taiwan.

category4.title: The future of robot armies is here – and it’s not what you think
category4.summary: Robots are becoming more a part of our lives every year, and
category4.detail: Robots are becoming more a part of our lives every year, and worries about a robot army rising up have long plagued the technology.
